data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260507T045037-3e188151/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260507T045037-3e188151/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260507T045037-3e188151/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260507T045037-3e188151/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260507T045037-3e188151/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

## Decomposition depth cap
This bead is already at decomposition depth 3. Do not create another child layer; if it is still too large, reject the split with a short explanation and write no_changes_rationale.txt instead.

  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-a06ef04a">
    <title>graphql: expose needs-human beads and review resolution</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
The web GUI cannot implement a Needs review filter or submit operator review decisions without GraphQL support. Existing bead queries and mutations expose generic lifecycle operations but not needs-human metadata or explicit review resolution.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:169-179 defines BeadStatusCounts without needsHuman or workerReady. cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:2263-2329 defines bead queries but no beadsNeedsHuman query. cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:3153-3188 defines bead mutations but no beadHumanResolve mutation. cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_beads.go:112-156 provides generic beadUpdate but not action-specific resolution with required notes.

PROPOSED FIX
Extend GraphQL with needs-human fields on Bead, needsHuman/workerReady counts, beadsNeedsHuman query, and beadHumanResolve mutation using action enum retry|split|obsolete|defer, required note, and optional children. Resolvers delegate to bead package helpers shared with CLI.

NON-SCOPE
Frontend rendering, execute-loop transition changes, or full artifact review chat.

DEPS
Depends on ddx-c5cab746.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. schema.graphql exposes Bead.needsHuman, Bead.needsHumanReason, Bead.needsHumanSuggestedAction, Bead.needsHumanSummary, BeadStatusCounts.needsHuman, BeadStatusCounts.workerReady, beadsNeedsHuman, and beadHumanResolve.
2. TestGraphQLBeadsNeedsHumanQuery verifies needs-human rows include reason/source/suggested action and exclude ordinary open beads.
3. TestGraphQLBeadHumanResolveRetry verifies retry resolution removes needs_human and appends a resolution event.
4. TestGraphQLBeadHumanResolveRequiresNote verifies all resolve actions reject empty notes.
5. TestGraphQLBeadStatusCountsNeedsHuman verifies counts match store.Status().
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/graphql/... green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:server, area:graphql, area:beads, kind:feature, operator-ux, needs-human-lane, spec:FEAT-008, spec:FEAT-010, triage:no-changes-unjustified</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-ac10e7af" spec-id="FEAT-008" base-rev="584082560596944e0381904bec42371419b0f853" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260507T045037-3e188151"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-008" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-web-ui.md">Feature: DDx Server Web UI</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output_summary: out=219B 3 lines "a41c06ce4 feat(graphql): expose needs-human b..."

data.output:
Done. The commit `a41c06ce4` completes bead `ddx-a06ef04a`. Here's a summary of what was delivered:

**Schema changes** (`schema.graphql`, `models.go`, `generated.go`):
- `Bead` type: added `needsHuman`, `needsHumanReason`, `needsHumanSuggestedAction`, `needsHumanSummary`
- `BeadStatusCounts` type: added `needsHuman`, `workerReady`
- New `beadsNeedsHuman` query returning a `BeadConnection` of open needs_human beads
- New `HumanResolveAction` enum: `retry | split | obsolete | defer`
- New `beadHumanResolve` mutation with required `note` and optional `children`

**Resolver implementations**:
- `BeadsNeedsHuman`: queries store directly for `needs_human`-labeled open beads, populates Extra fields
- `BeadsStatus`: uses `store.Status()` + separate counts for `needsHuman` and `workerReady`
- `BeadHumanResolve`: `retry`/`defer` removes the label; `obsolete`/`split` closes the bead; all actions append a `human-resolution` event; empty note is rejected
- `beadFromSnapshot` and `beadModelFromBead` now populate `NeedsHuman` from labels and Extra fields

**Tests** (`resolver_beads_needs_human_test.go`): all 4 required tests pass.
